text: Hi, Simon. My name is Alison and I'm calling from First Union Bank. How are you doing today? Hey, Alison. Everything is fine. How are you? I'm good, thanks. I just need to quickly verify that I'm speaking to the correct person. Can you please confirm the last four digits of your Social Security number? Um, uh, that's actually personal information. What is this for? This is an important call about your credit card, and it will only take a few minutes. Is that okay? Yeah. Uh, that's okay. Great. And I'll just need the last four digits of your Social Security number. Um, okay. Uh, that will be 8469. Great. Thank you. Simon, I'm calling about your outstanding credit card balance of $4,829 that has been overdue for 30 days. Now, I would like to know if I can take this payment for this amount over the phone today. Um, uh, wait, wait. Um, what was that amount again? The outstanding amount is $4,829. Wow. Um, uh, I'm actually waiting on a paycheck this month. I can't pay this as of right now. Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. Well, Simon, in order to prevent damage to your credit history, I do recommend that you make at least a partial payment today. Is that possible? I really don't know. What does a partial payment mean? Um, the minimum amount is 25% of the outstanding amount. Will that work for you? Yes, that works. That's great. You can pay either with a bank transfer, another credit or debit card. Which one do you prefer? Um, I, I actually prefer the bank transfer. Bank transfer? Okay. I can text you our bank details. Is that okay? Yeah, that'll be perfect. Excellent. Well, I just sent you the details. Okay, Simon? Thank you for your time, and we're available if you need anything else or if you have problems with the payment. Have a great day. Thank you. Bye.
